title: Self-Compassion Practice for Perfectionists
description: A daily practice guide for building self-compassion as an alternative to perfectionist self-criticism. Use this checklist to track your self-compassion practice across the three core components: self-kindness, common humanity, and mindfulness.
instructions: Review each item at the end of your day. Check off any practices you engaged in today. Aim to practice at least a few items from each category regularly — perfection is not the goal.

items.label: I noticed when my inner critic was active today
I paused before reacting to a self-critical thought
I named what I was feeling without judging it (e.g., 'I notice I feel anxious')
I spoke to myself with kind or encouraging words after a setback
I asked myself 'What would I say to a friend in this situation?' and said it to myself
I used a physical gesture of comfort (hand on heart, gentle touch, warm drink)
I allowed myself to rest without guilt
I reminded myself that struggling is a normal part of being human
I recognized that others face similar challenges, even if they do not show it
I accepted an imperfection without trying to fix it
I used the self-compassion break (mindfulness, common humanity, self-kindness phrases)
I replaced a harsh self-judgment with a balanced, compassionate perspective
I acknowledged my effort or progress, even if the result was not perfect

disclaimer: This checklist is for educational purposes and personal growth. It is not a substitute for professional mental health care. If you are struggling significantly, please consider speaking with a qualified mental health professional.
